title: lathyrus annuus var. hierosolymitanus
intro: Lathyrus annuus var. hierosolymitanus, also known as the Jerusalem pea, is a variety of the annual pea plant, characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and edible seeds. The seeds of Lathyrus annuus var. hierosolymitanus contain a compound called ODAP, which has been reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming the seeds in moderation has been associated with various health benefits, such as reduced inflammation and improved cardiovascular health, although excessive consumption may cause health issues due to the high ODAP content. In herbal preparations, Lathyrus annuus var. hierosolymitanus seeds are often used in traditional medicine, typically in small amounts, and may be consumed as a tea or added to supplements after proper processing to minimize the risks associated with ODAP.
